chosen_logo.why: the-coca-cola-company-logo.svg from corporate site header — direct SVG URL of the Spencerian wordmark, hosted by Coca-Cola itself. SVG vector means clean currentColor flip between white-on-black and red-on-white.

brand_primary.name: --color-brand-red
brand_primary.evidence: Coca-Cola Red — the iconic colour that IS the brand. Per user direction: this is the dominant chord, used as cover background. Captured as `--color-icon-brand-primary-regular: #ea0000` in the site's design-token system. (User chose 'full red bg + white wordmark' over the corporate-site monochrome reading.)

brand_secondary.name: --color-brand-red
brand_secondary.evidence: Coca-Cola has a single brand colour. --primary AND --accent both map to Coca-Cola Red — covers use it as bg, content slides use it as CTA / chart fill / pull-quote border.

ink.name: --color-brand-ink
ink.evidence: Pure black for body text — 674× in CSS. Coca-Cola has chosen unsentimental pure black, not Apple-Ink-style off-black.

paper.name: --color-brand-paper
paper.evidence: Computed body bg rgb(238,238,238) = #EEEEEE on every page. Coca-Cola uses a light-grey paper rather than pure white — gives the editorial feel without surface glare.

accents.name: --color-brand-red-deep
accents.evidence: `--color-icon-component-input-critical: #bf1004` — deeper red used for input-critical states. The shadow-mode of the brand red.

accents.name: --color-brand-red-pale
accents.evidence: Pale red wash — 6× in CSS. Soft callout / row-tint variant of the brand red.

accents.name: --color-brand-coral
accents.evidence: Warm coral — 30× in CSS. Used for soft-CTA variants and hover transitions.

accents.name: --color-brand-paper-pure
accents.evidence: Pure white — 622× in CSS. Used as the on-black surface and inverse paper for high-contrast moments.

neutrals.name: --color-brand-graphite
neutrals.evidence: Mid-tone grey — 80× in CSS. Used for secondary text and disabled states.

neutrals.name: --color-brand-stone
neutrals.evidence: Light grey — 240× in CSS. Used as `--color-icon-action-on_brand-primary-disabled` and divider lines.

neutrals.name: --color-brand-coal
neutrals.evidence: Near-black — 78× in CSS. Used for hover states on dark surfaces.

display_font.evidence: User chose Georgia as serif fallback for Coca-Cola's editorial register (TCCC-UnityText is proprietary, not licensable for slide decks). Pairs the 1886-brand provenance with magazine-newspaper typography.

body_font.evidence: User chose Helvetica Neue as sans body to complement Georgia headlines — classic editorial pairing.

spacing_radius_shadow.radius_style: split — sharp 0px on surfaces, 8000px (full pill) on CTAs
spacing_radius_shadow.shadow_style: flat — no box-shadow on top-level chrome
spacing_radius_shadow.evidence: Computed surface radius 0px on body / h1; button radius 8000px (effectively pill — same logic as 999px). No shadows observed. Body padX/padY varies but never tight.

aesthetic.mood: editorial monochrome with iconic red wordmark
precedents: New York Times feature pages — but with the iconic Coke wordmark as the brand exclamation
Harvard Business Review covers — black on grey with one accent
Apple's monochrome treatment but with red instead of blue
1960s magazine ad layouts — wordmark + manifesto copy + generous whitespace
aesthetic.voice_register: corporate editorial; manifesto-grade copy with the iconic wordmark as the visual anchor. The brand voice on the site is quiet — the wordmark does the heavy lifting.
